# Performance Checklist: LSP Client Integration

**Purpose**: Validate SPEC-008 performance requirements for opt-in LSP precision, disabled-path overhead, bounded server work, watch behavior, large-repo behavior, and retrieval sufficiency.
**Created**: 2026-07-05
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are zero-overhead requirements specified for disabled LSP precision, including no server probing, no subprocess startup, no LSP status writes, and unchanged graph behavior? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Completeness, Spec §FR-042, Spec §SC-011, Plan §Performance Budgets and Large-Repo Behavior]
- [x] CHK002 Are index-time overhead requirements specified for LSP-enabled full indexing with measurable default budgets and evidence requirements? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Completeness, Spec §FR-045, Spec §SC-012, Plan §Performance Budgets and Large-Repo Behavior]
- [x] CHK003 Are per-language work caps specified for full-index LSP verification, not only watch batches? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Completeness, Spec §FR-043, Contract §Performance Bounds]
- [x] CHK004 Are language-server session concurrency limits specified so large repositories cannot start unbounded parallel LSP subprocesses or requests? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Completeness, Spec §FR-044, Contract §Performance Bounds]
- [ ] CHK005 Are timeout, restart, and degraded-language limits specified as bounded runtime controls rather than open-ended retries? [Completeness, Spec §FR-038-FR-038a, Plan §Runtime Restart and Retry Policy]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is "zero overhead" defined with objective evidence for the disabled path rather than only graph-output equivalence? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Clarity, Spec §FR-042, Spec §SC-011, Data Model §LspPerformanceRecord]
- [ ] CHK007 Is "bounded per-language work" quantified for index, sync, and watch paths with separate file, candidate, and request limits? [Clarity, Spec §FR-035a, Plan §Activation, Config, and Watch Contract]
- [ ] CHK008 Are request timeout values and timeout precedence clear enough for implementers to avoid language-specific drift? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005-FR-006, Spec §Configuration Contract, Plan §Activation, Config, and Watch Contract]
- [ ] CHK009 Are skip/degrade reason codes defined for performance bounds such as oversized full-index language work and oversized watch batches? [Clarity, Spec §FR-031a, Spec §FR-035a]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK010 Do performance requirements preserve the local-first and default-off constraints without adding auto-install, remote calls, or implicit activation? [Consistency, Spec §FR-020-FR-024, Constitution §VII]
- [ ] CHK011 Are watch bounds consistent across spec, plan, config contract, and data model records? [Consistency, Spec §FR-035-FR-035a, Plan §Activation, Config, and Watch Contract, Data Model §EdgeVerificationWorkItem]
- [ ] CHK012 Are retry limits consistent between normal indexing and bounded watch verification so server recovery cannot loop across debounce cycles? [Consistency, Spec §FR-038-FR-038a, Plan §Runtime Restart and Retry Policy]

## Large-Repo And Retrieval Sufficiency

- [x] CHK013 Are large-repo validation requirements specified for representative small, medium, and large repositories or fixtures when `--lsp` is enabled? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Coverage, Spec §FR-047, Spec §SC-013, Plan §Validation Strategy]
- [x] CHK014 Are retrieval sufficiency requirements measurable enough to prevent regressions in `codegraph_explore`, callers, callees, impact, search, and flow-building surfaces? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Measurability, Spec §FR-046, Spec §SC-014, Plan §Validation Strategy]
- [ ] CHK015 Are node/edge-count stability requirements specified for LSP-enabled correction and suppression without allowing performance-costly graph noise? [Completeness, Spec §SC-010, Contract §Invariants]

## Incremental Watch Performance

- [ ] CHK016 Are incremental watch requirements explicit that LSP verification runs only after normal sync/reference resolution and never falls back to a repository-wide LSP pass? [Completeness, Spec §FR-035-FR-035a]
- [ ] CHK017 Are changed-file and per-language candidate caps measurable enough to bound watch-event cost? [Measurability, Spec §FR-035a, Plan §Activation, Config, and Watch Contract]
- [ ] CHK018 Are repeated watch debounce cycles prevented from resetting exhausted server restart budgets for the same failed changed-file set? [Reliability, Spec §FR-038a, Plan §Runtime Restart and Retry Policy]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK019 Are success criteria measurable for disabled-path zero overhead, enabled-path bounded overhead, large-repo behavior, and retrieval sufficiency? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-011-SC-014]
- [x] CHK020 Is final validation evidence required to include both non-LSP performance preservation and LSP-enabled bounded-work performance results? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Acceptance Criteria, Plan §Validation Strategy, Research §Decision: Use fixed SPEC-008 LSP performance caps before adding user-facing tuning]

## Re-run Assessment

- [x] CHK021 Are full-index source-file, work-item, batch-size, session, and request caps aligned across spec, plan, contracts, and data model? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Consistency, Spec §FR-043-FR-045, Plan §Performance Budgets and Large-Repo Behavior, Contract §Performance Bounds, Data Model §LspPerformanceRecord]
- [x] CHK022 Are disabled-path zero-runtime-work requirements measurable without relying only on graph-output equivalence? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Measurability, Spec §FR-042, Spec §SC-011, Data Model §LspPerformanceRecord]
- [x] CHK023 Are large-repo and retrieval sufficiency requirements traceable to validation evidence rather than implied by general performance goals? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Traceability, Spec §FR-046-FR-047, Spec §SC-013-SC-014, Plan §Validation Strategy]
- [x] CHK024 Are performance skip/degrade reasons specified for exceeded full-index and watch bounds without permitting an unbounded repository-wide fallback? [Resolved 2026-07-05, Coverage, Spec §FR-035a, Spec §FR-043, Contract §Performance Bounds]

## Notes

- Focus areas selected: timeout budgets, concurrency limits, per-language work caps, index-time overhead, disabled-path overhead, incremental watch verification, large-repo behavior, and retrieval sufficiency.
- Depth level: Standard reviewer checklist for Phase 4 performance readiness.
- Actor/timing: PR reviewer during SPEC-008 Phase 4 checklist gate.
- Explicit must-have items incorporated: timeout budgets, concurrency limits, per-language work caps, index-time overhead with `--lsp`, zero overhead when disabled, incremental verification on watch events, large-repo behavior, and no regression to retrieval tool sufficiency.
- Remediation rerun: CHK001-CHK004, CHK006, CHK013-CHK014, and CHK019-CHK020 were re-evaluated after spec/plan/research/data-model/contract updates on 2026-07-05; no performance gaps remain.
